display.purpose: Hero headlines on brand-register surfaces (Bricolage Grotesque).

headline.purpose: Section-level headings on brand-register surfaces (Bricolage Grotesque).

title.purpose: Page-level headings in the product register, and every shadcn Title role (Card/Dialog/Sheet/Drawer), now rendered in Bricolage Grotesque via --font-heading.

body.purpose: Default UI text, labels, card content.

shadows.name: overlay
shadows.purpose: Dialogs, sheets, popovers, dropdowns, select menus — anything rendered in a portal above the page.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Default call-to-action button; Growth Green fill.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; height: 32px; padding: 0 10px; border-radius: 8px; border: 1px solid transparent; background: oklch(0.8506 0.2199 152.5506); color: oklch(0.2626 0.0147 166.4589); font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; transition: background 0.15s, transform 0.1s; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: color-mix(in oklch, oklch(0.8506 0.2199 152.5506) 80%, transparent); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px oklch(0.8348 0.1302 160.908 / 0.5); } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: translateY(1px); }

components.name: Outline Button
components.description: Secondary action button; transparent with hairline border.
components.css: .ds-btn-outline { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; height: 32px; padding: 0 10px; border-radius: 8px; border: 1px solid oklch(0.9037 0 0); background: oklch(0.9911 0 0); color: oklch(0.2046 0 0); font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; transition: background 0.15s; } .ds-btn-outline:hover { background: oklch(0.9461 0 0); } .ds-btn-outline:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px oklch(0.8348 0.1302 160.908 / 0.5); }

components.name: Card
components.description: Flat container with a hairline ring instead of a shadow.
components.html:
Monthly spending
You're on track this month.
components.css: .ds-card { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 16px; border-radius: 12px; background: oklch(0.9911 0 0); padding: 16px; box-shadow: 0 0 0 1px oklch(0.2046 0 0 / 0.1); } .ds-card-title { font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 500; color: oklch(0.2046 0 0); } .ds-card-body { font-size: 0.875rem; color: oklch(0.2046 0 0 / 0.6); }

components.name: Input
components.description: Text field with hairline border and Growth-Green focus ring.
components.css: .ds-input { height: 32px; width: 100%; border-radius: 8px; border: 1px solid oklch(0.9037 0 0); background: transparent; padding: 4px 10px; font-size: 0.875rem; color: oklch(0.2046 0 0); } .ds-input::placeholder { color: oklch(0.2435 0 0 / 0.65); } .ds-input:focus-visible { outline: none; border-color: oklch(0.8348 0.1302 160.908); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px oklch(0.8348 0.1302 160.908 / 0.5); }

components.name: Primary Badge
components.description: Pill-shaped status/label chip in the primary accent.
components.css: .ds-badge-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; height: 20px; padding: 2px 8px; border-radius: 9999px; background: oklch(0.8506 0.2199 152.5506); color: oklch(0.2626 0.0147 166.4589); font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 500; }

narrative.overview: SmartBudget's product surface doesn't perform difficulty. Surfaces are flat and near-silent — a near-white canvas, near-black text, a single hairline ring standing in for borders — so that the one color the system permits itself, a fresh mint growth-green, reads as a genuine signal rather than decoration. The system earns trust by getting out of the way: no gradients, no ambient shadows on resting surfaces, no competing accents. Depth is reserved for things that are actually elevated off the page (menus, dialogs, sheets); everything else sits flat against the canvas. This rejects the generic-fintech look explicitly — no navy-and-gold, no corporate stock-photo chrome, no manufactured urgency. It also rejects card-on-card nesting and gratuitous elevation: a card is a hairline ring, not a drop shadow, and it never contains another card.
keyCharacteristics: One accent, used deliberately: growth-green marks primary actions, positive state, and brand identity — nowhere else.
Flat by default: cards, inputs, and buttons rest without shadow; only floating layers cast one.
A deliberate display role now exists: Bricolage Grotesque carries hero and section headlines on brand-register surfaces, layered over the same system-sans body.
Tight, consistent radius scale: 8px for interactive controls, 12px for containers.

rules.name: The One Signal Rule
rules.body: Growth Green is the only saturated color allowed outside chart contexts. If a second accent color feels necessary anywhere in the UI, the answer is a neutral (Quiet Gray) or an opacity of the existing ink, not a new hue.

rules.name: The One Display Family Rule
rules.body: Bricolage Grotesque is the only display face in the system, used for Display and Headline roles on brand surfaces and for the Title role everywhere. Don't introduce a second display family for a future surface; extend weight/size within this one instead.

rules.name: The Floating-Only Rule
rules.body: If a component renders inline in the document flow, it does not get a shadow — a Hairline ring is the ceiling. Shadows are earned exclusively by leaving the page's stacking context.

dos: Do keep Growth Green as the only saturated UI color; everything else is neutral or a chart-only hue.
Do use a Hairline ring (ring-1 ring-foreground/10), not a shadow, for any surface that rests in the page's normal flow.
Do reserve real box-shadows for components that render in a portal above the page (dialogs, sheets, popovers, menus).
Do keep the 8px/12px radius relationship: controls at 8px, containers one step up at 12px.
Do use the Display/Headline Bricolage Grotesque roles for any new brand-register surface's headings; don't scale up Title or introduce a second display face.
donts: Don't build a generic-fintech look: no navy-and-gold palette, no corporate stock photography, no enterprise-dashboard chrome.
Don't add a second saturated accent color outside chart contexts to "add warmth" or "add energy" — Growth Green carries all of the brand's color weight by design.
Don't nest cards inside cards, or add a shadow to a resting card to make it feel "lifted."
Don't use border-left or border-right as a colored accent stripe on cards or callouts.
Don't use background-clip: text gradient text for emphasis — weight and size carry emphasis in this system.
